mnemonics: CIA (Create, Insert, Access) - The core operations you need to manage your data structures.
keyPoints: The five fundamental data structure operations are: Creation, Insertion, Deletion, Searching/Traversal, and Updating/Accessing.
Pointers store memory addresses, enabling dynamic data structures and efficient data manipulation.
Dynamic Memory Allocation (malloc, calloc, free) allows programs to request and release memory at runtime, which is essential for building flexible data structures like linked lists and trees.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to free dynamically allocated memory, leading to memory leaks.
Dereferencing a NULL or uninitialized pointer, which causes segmentation faults.
Using a pointer after the memory it points to has been freed (dangling pointer), resulting in undefined behavior.
